channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers withdraw plans for psychiatric defense
item.description: In a court filing Thursday, Mangione's legal team said they won't file psychiatric evidence in the 28-year-old's state murder case. The move came a day after his lawyers said they planned to pursue a psychiatric defense.
item.content:encoded:
In a court filing Thursday, Mangione's legal team said they won't file psychiatric evidence in the 28-year-old's state murder case. The move came a day after his lawyers said they planned to pursue a psychiatric defense.

(Image credit: Angelina Katsanis)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Mississippi authorities investigate police shooting that left a 1-year-old dead
item.description: Authorities in Mississippi are investigating a police shooting that left a young child dead and an adult seriously injured when police shot at a car in a parking lot during an alleged shoplifting call.
item.content:encoded: Authorities in Mississippi are investigating a police shooting that left a young child dead and an adult seriously injured when police shot at a car in a parking lot during an alleged shoplifting call.
item.dc:creator: Elise Gregg

item.title: Student loan borrowers will get an interest rate cut if they sign up for auto pay
item.description: The Trump administration wants to jumpstart student loan repayment, with federal student loan debt approaching $2 trillion.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration wants to jumpstart student loan repayment, with federal student loan debt approaching $2 trillion.

(Image credit: Daniel de la Hoz)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Read the full text of Trump's preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement to end the war
item.description: Here is the text of the memorandum of understanding that was signed Wednesday by President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, as well as Pakistan's prime minister.
item.content:encoded:
Here is the text of the memorandum of understanding that was signed Wednesday by President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, as well as Pakistan's prime minister.

(Image credit: Nathan Laine)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Supreme Court sides with a marijuana user who was barred from owning guns
item.description: The court ruled that the law used to prosecute a marijuana user violated his Second Amendment right to bear arms and is unconstitutionally vague.
item.content:encoded:
The court ruled that the law used to prosecute a marijuana user violated his Second Amendment right to bear arms and is unconstitutionally vague.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Grady Martin

item.title: Embedded: "We Keep Us Safe" from NPR, KUOW and The Seattle Times
item.description: In the summer of 2020, sixteen-year-old Antonio Mays Jr. traveled a thousand miles to join the racial justice movement of his generation. He arrived in Seattle during the Capitol Hill Occupied Protest, known as CHOP. Less than a week later, he was shot and killed there. The case remains unsolved.
item.content:encoded: In the summer of 2020, sixteen-year-old Antonio Mays Jr. traveled a thousand miles to join the racial justice movement of his generation. He arrived in Seattle during the Capitol Hill Occupied Protest, known as CHOP. Less than a week later, he was shot and killed there. The case remains unsolved.

item.title: Report: Russia's nuclear-powered 'Skyfall' missile is dirty and dangerous
item.description: MIT researchers think they've worked out exactly how Russia's Burevestnik nuclear-powered missile flies. "It's almost certainly a terrible idea," one analyst said. "But it's not an impossible idea."
item.content:encoded:
MIT researchers think they've worked out exactly how Russia's Burevestnik nuclear-powered missile flies. "It's almost certainly a terrible idea," one analyst said. "But it's not an impossible idea."

(Image credit: Russian Ministry of Defense)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: What — and who — will be at the Great American State Fair? Here's a primer
item.description: Several state governments have declined to participate in the 16-day event, though organizers say all U.S. states and territories will be represented by booths on the National Mall.
item.content:encoded:
Several state governments have declined to participate in the 16-day event, though organizers say all U.S. states and territories will be represented by booths on the National Mall.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: The price of jet fuel is falling, but don't expect airfares to follow any time soon
item.description: The average price of jet fuel has fallen to its lowest level since the beginning of the war with Iran. But aviation experts say the cost of airfare is likely to stay high, at least for now.
item.content:encoded:
The average price of jet fuel has fallen to its lowest level since the beginning of the war with Iran. But aviation experts say the cost of airfare is likely to stay high, at least for now.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Poll: Most Americans have the summer blues about Trump and the economy
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.
item.content:encoded:
A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.

(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler
